name: Chick-Peas in Chunky Tomato Sauce Over Cousco
recipeIngredient: 1 can (28 oz) Italian Plums tomatoes with juices
1 can (15-16 oz) Garbanzo beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large non-stick skillet, combine chopped onions, chopped carrot, 1 tbsp minced garlic, and 1 tsp olive oil.
2. Cover and cook the mixture over low heat until vegetables are tender, stirring frequently for about 15 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes with juices, garbanzo beans, oregano, thyme, and red pepper to the skillet. Bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Stir the mixture, crushing the tomatoes with the side of a spoon. Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered until the sauce thickens and flavors blend for about 20 minutes.
5. Add parsley. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
6. (Can be prepared one day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Rewarm over low heat before continuing.)
7. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a heavy medium saucepan. Stir in couscous. Remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 10 minutes.
8. Fluff the couscous with a fork. Heat remaining 3 teaspoons olive oil in a heavy skillet over low heat.
9. Add the remaining 1 teaspoon minced garlic and cook for 1 minute. Stir the garlic and oil mixture into the couscous.
10. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Spoon couscous into shallow bowls, ladle the tomato mixture over, and serve.
